company_core.products: OctoAI offers a generative AI inference platform designed for efficient and customizable model serving, leveraging technologies such as XG Boost, TVM, and MLC to optimize enterprise performance. The platform supports automated deployment and tuning of models across diverse hardware environments, enhancing the scalability of generative AI applications.

founders.name: Luis Ceze
founders.background:
Luis Ceze is a seasoned entrepreneur and academic with a PhD in Computer Science from the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign. He co-founded and served as Technical Advisor at CORENSIC from 2008 to 2012 and was a Venture Partner at Madrona from 2018 to 2024. Additionally, he has been a professor at the University of Washington since 2007, focusing on computer systems architecture and machine learning.

---
Sources: linkedin.com/in/luis-ceze-50b2314, x.com/luisceze

founders.name: Tianqi Chen
founders.background:
Tianqi Chen is a former Assistant Professor at Carnegie Mellon University, where he focused on machine learning and data mining. He co-founded OctoAI, serving as Chief Technologist and CTO from 2019 to 2024, leading the company until its acquisition by NVIDIA. Chen holds a PhD in Computer Science from the University of Washington and a BS in Computer Science from Shanghai Jiao Tong University.

---
Sources: linkedin.com/in/tianqi-chen-679a9856, twitter.com/tqchenml

founders.name: Jared Roesch
founders.background:
Jared Roesch holds a Ph.D. in Computer Science & Engineering from the University of Washington and has significant contributions to the open-source community, notably as a committer and PMC member at Apache TVM since 2017, where he was the second-largest contributor. He also served as a committer for the Rust compiler from 2014 to 2016, focusing on the type system and error handling. Prior to co-founding OctoAI, he was the Chief Technology Officer, leading the company through a successful product transition and exit.

---
Sources: linkedin.com/in/jaredroesch, x.com/roeschinc

founders.name: Dipankar Sarkar
founders.background:
Dipankar Sarkar has over 14 years of experience in the tech industry, including significant roles such as AI lead at MFD and Head of AI & Platform at Orangewood, where he worked on innovative projects involving Large Language Models and deep learning. He founded Cryptuon Research, focusing on Web3 technologies, and served as AI & ML Lead at Hike, contributing to its rapid ascent to unicorn status. Dipankar holds a B.Tech in Computer Science from the Indian Institute of Technology, Delhi, and a Master of Science in Computer Science from Arizona State University.

---
Sources: linkedin.com/in/dipankarsarkar, twitter.com/dipankarsarkar

news.article_headline: What the NVIDIA acquisition of OctoAI means for you
news.excerpts: What the NVIDIA acquisition of OctoAI means for you  What was OctoAI?. At its heart, OctoAI was an inference platform made for developers. Its main product, OctoStack, was a full toolkit for deploying, running, and scaling generative AI models. Whether you were using a popular open-source model like Llama or a custom-built one, OctoAI provided the engine to get it into production.. It tackled a huge headache for ML engineers and developers: how to manage the intense computing power AI needs...

agent_output.subject: OctoAI's inference optimization stack
agent_output.message:
Hey Luis,

I'm an investor and engineer focused on data and AI infra at NEA, where we've backed companies like Perplexity, Databricks, and Together AI.

I've been following OctoAI's work on inference optimization, particularly your use of TVM and MLC for automated model tuning across heterogeneous hardware. The approach to compilation-based optimization for generative models is really compelling, especially as enterprises struggle with the cost and latency tradeoffs of serving large models at scale.

I'd love to learn more about where you're taking the platform and your long-term vision for inference infrastructure as model architectures continue to evolve.

Do you have any availability in the coming weeks to chat?

Best,
Ashley Jepson
